queries.q: I need to add an endpoint that fetches a user's billing history.

atoms.summary: NEVER create module-level SQLAlchemy sessions or share sessions across requests. Sessions must be request-scoped via Depends(get_session) or Depends(get_async_session).
atoms.rationale: PR #14 fixes routes/legacy.py module-level session that caused: (1) concurrent requests sharing transaction state, (2) connection leaks causing 'too many connections' alerts in staging, (3) unbounded identity map growth leading to OOM. Sessions are not thread/task-safe; one request's rollback affects another's writes.

atoms.summary: Do not use utils/db_helper; use Depends(get_session) or Depends(get_async_session) instead. Module raises RuntimeError after 2027-Q1.
atoms.rationale: PR #8 deprecates db_helper after a staging incident where concurrent calls exhausted the connection pool (20 conn limit), stalling the app for 90s. Injected sessions enforce pool limits and per-request lifecycle. Grace window until 2027-Q1 for external forks to migrate.

atoms.summary: Use Result<T> for expected business failures; middleware can raise exceptions, domain handlers must return Result<T>.
atoms.rationale: Q3 incident: async exception propagation inside asyncio.gather silently dropped distributed-tracing spans. Establishes explicit exception boundary: middleware (infra-level) can raise, domain handlers (business logic) must use Result<T> for consistency and tracing compatibility. Exceptions reserved for truly exceptional conditions (DB unreachable, kernel panic, config error).

atoms.summary: Do not bypass get_session because 'the session is too short-lived'; fix the route lifecycle instead.
atoms.rationale: Bypassing the injected session pool prevents enforcement of connection-pool limits and per-request lifecycle, leading to connection exhaustion.

atoms.summary: CI must fail the build if any FastAPI route handler awaits known-async functions without being async def.
atoms.rationale: PR #11 adds pytest-collect hook (check_async_routes.py) to enforce async-first mechanically and prevent drift over time. Without CI check, the policy erodes in 6 months.

queries.q: How should I handle errors in route handlers?

atoms.summary: Use Result<T> for expected business failures; middleware can raise exceptions, domain handlers must return Result<T>.
atoms.rationale: Q3 incident: async exception propagation inside asyncio.gather silently dropped distributed-tracing spans. Establishes explicit exception boundary: middleware (infra-level) can raise, domain handlers (business logic) must use Result<T> for consistency and tracing compatibility. Exceptions reserved for truly exceptional conditions (DB unreachable, kernel panic, config error).

atoms.summary: Route handlers that perform I/O (DB, HTTP, queue, file) MUST be async def; pure-compute routes can remain def.
atoms.rationale: PR #11 locks async-first policy. Sync handlers block workers on I/O, capping throughput at ~270 req/s; async lifts that ~3x. Also enables asyncio.gather fan-out and avoids sync-async footguns. Load testing shows ~3x improvement with 8 workers and 30ms DB latency.

atoms.summary: CI must fail the build if any FastAPI route handler awaits known-async functions without being async def.
atoms.rationale: PR #11 adds pytest-collect hook (check_async_routes.py) to enforce async-first mechanically and prevent drift over time. Without CI check, the policy erodes in 6 months.

atoms.summary: CI and pre-commit hooks must AST-check for SessionLocal() or async_sessionmaker()() calls outside function scope and fail the build.
atoms.rationale: PR #14 adds tools/lint_session_check.py to mechanically prevent module-level session anti-pattern from reoccurring. Catches violations at PR time before runtime, preventing recurrence of connection-leak incidents.

queries.q: Can I cache user-specific responses globally?

atoms.summary: Cache keys must include user identity if the response depends on requesting user's permissions.
atoms.rationale: PR #10 review highlights auth risk: cached objects that depend on user permissions must include user in cache key to avoid unauthorized data leakage. Confirm independence before using user-agnostic keys.
